description: PublishRequest containing a producer-supplied `created_at` MUST be rejected with schema_violation (HTTP 400). `created_at` is registry-assigned (RFC-ACDP-0003 §2.1 step 8) at canonical millisecond precision (RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.3) and is in the §5.7 exclusion set. The publish-request schema is closed; a registry MUST NOT silently strip or echo a producer-supplied timestamp, because doing so creates ambiguity about which clock authored the recorded value.

input.notes: Same closed-schema rejection applies to producer-supplied `origin_registry`. This fixture covers `created_at` specifically because incorrect timestamp handling has the highest auditability impact: a body recorded at the producer's clock value rather than the registry's would silently break ordering invariants. `pub-013` covers `ctx_id`; consumers MAY synthesize a sister fixture for `origin_registry` and `lineage_id` (when `version = 1`) — the schema enforces all of them by virtue of `additionalProperties: false`.
